title: prunella vulgaris subsp. estremadurensis
intro: Prunella vulgaris subsp. estremadurensis, also known as the self-heal, is a subspecies of the Prunella vulgaris plant, a member of the mint family. This herb is used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral effects, which can be used to treat various health issues such as wounds, digestive problems, and respiratory infections. The health benefits of self-heal include reducing stress, improving sleep quality, and boosting the immune system, making it a popular choice for herbal remedies. Self-heal can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is also used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and antiviral properties.
